name: Arjun Sharma
suggestedActions: Schedule urgent cardiology consultation within 48 hours
Increase Metoprolol dosage to 100mg — confirm with cardiologist
Recommend DASH diet: reduce sodium intake to <2g/day
Prescribe cardiac rehabilitation program (3 sessions/week)
Order repeat echocardiogram in 4 weeks
Monitor daily BP at home — log readings via app

historyTimeline.title: Cardiac Assessment
historyTimeline.summary: EF dropped to 38%, BP elevated. ECG showed mild ST depression. Medication adjusted.

historyTimeline.title: Follow-up Consultation
historyTimeline.summary: Patient reported chest tightness. Stress test performed. Moderate exertional angina noted.

historyTimeline.title: Routine Checkup
historyTimeline.summary: Cholesterol elevated at 245mg/dL. Statin therapy initiated. BP at 142/90.

historyTimeline.title: Initial Diagnosis
historyTimeline.summary: Diagnosed with ischemic heart disease. Started on aspirin, beta-blocker therapy.

llmSummary: Patient Arjun Sharma, 58M, presents with advanced ischemic heart disease. Recent echocardiogram reveals significantly reduced ejection fraction (38%), indicating moderate-to-severe systolic dysfunction. Blood pressure remains critically elevated (148/96 mmHg) despite ongoing antihypertensive therapy. Immediate escalation of cardiac intervention is recommended.

name: Meera Krishnamurthy
suggestedActions: Optimize insulin regimen — consider switching to basal-bolus therapy
Refer to diabetes educator for carb counting training
Annual retinal screening — schedule with ophthalmology
Foot examination for early neuropathy signs
Monitor kidney function with quarterly creatinine/eGFR

historyTimeline.title: Diabetes Review
historyTimeline.summary: HbA1c increased to 8.2%. Medication adjusted. Patient advised dietary compliance.

historyTimeline.title: Quarterly Follow-up
historyTimeline.summary: HbA1c at 7.8%. Good dietary compliance noted. Foot exam normal.

historyTimeline.title: Endocrinology Consult
historyTimeline.summary: Type 2 DM management optimized. Metformin 1g BD continued. Added Sitagliptin.

llmSummary: Patient Meera Krishnamurthy, 45F, with Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus showing suboptimal glycemic control. HbA1c of 8.2% indicates above-target glucose levels. Post-prandial spikes are a major concern. Ophthalmological and podiatric screening are overdue.

name: Venkatesh Iyer
suggestedActions: URGENT: Evaluate for hypertensive emergency — BP 168/104
Add amlodipine 5mg to current regimen immediately
24-hour ambulatory BP monitoring (ABPM)
Check for end-organ damage: renal function, fundoscopy
Strict sodium restriction (<1.5g/day)
Supplement potassium (KCl) — monitor levels weekly

historyTimeline.title: Hypertension Crisis Review
historyTimeline.summary: BP 168/104, highest recorded. Adjusted medications. Admitted for observation.

historyTimeline.title: Monthly BP Check
historyTimeline.summary: BP stabilizing at 145/92. Medication compliance confirmed. Diet advice given.

llmSummary: Patient Venkatesh Iyer, 62M, with Stage 2 hypertension and early CKD (eGFR 62). BP dangerously elevated at 168/104 mmHg — hypertensive urgency. Potassium low, possibly diuretic-induced hypokalemia. Immediate medication adjustment and close monitoring required.

name: Lakshmi Patel
suggestedActions: Initiate CBT (Cognitive Behavioral Therapy) sessions — 8 weeks
Prescribe mindfulness-based stress reduction (MBSR) program
Low-dose SSRI consideration — requires psychiatrist referral
Encourage regular aerobic exercise: 30 min, 5 days/week
Sleep hygiene counseling — target 7-8 hours quality sleep

historyTimeline.title: Stress & Mental Health Review
historyTimeline.summary: GAD-7 score of 14 indicates moderate anxiety. Cortisol elevated. CBT recommended.

historyTimeline.title: Initial Psychiatric Screening
historyTimeline.summary: Occupational stress identified as primary trigger. Work-life balance counseling initiated.

llmSummary: Patient Lakshmi Patel, 35F, with moderate anxiety disorder. GAD-7 score of 14. Sleep quality significantly impaired. Cortisol mildly elevated. No cardiovascular or metabolic comorbidities. Psychological interventions are the primary recommended treatment.

name: Ravi Chandrasekaran
suggestedActions: CRITICAL: Consider immediate hospital admission — check for ACS
12-lead ECG urgently — rule out STEMI/NSTEMI
Cardiology consult within 2 hours
Start dual antiplatelet therapy (aspirin + clopidogrel)
Supplemental oxygen therapy for SpO2 < 94%
Serial troponin measurements every 3 hours

historyTimeline.title: CRITICAL: Possible ACS
historyTimeline.summary: Troponin elevated, SpO2 dropping. Possible acute coronary syndrome. Hospitalization under review.

historyTimeline.title: Post-Stent Follow-up
historyTimeline.summary: 6-month post-PCI follow-up. Dual antiplatelet therapy continued. EF stable at 45%.

llmSummary: Patient Ravi Chandrasekaran, 70M, post-PCI with acute decompensation. Elevated troponin (0.12 ng/mL), elevated BNP (520 pg/mL), critically low SpO2 (93%). This is a medical emergency requiring immediate hospitalization and cardiology intervention.

name: Divya Menon
suggestedActions: Continue current Metformin 500mg BD — excellent response
Maintain Mediterranean diet — positive outcomes
Annual retinal screening scheduled
Continue regular 45-min daily walks
Next HbA1c check in 3 months

historyTimeline.title: Excellent Diabetes Control
historyTimeline.summary: HbA1c improved to 6.8% — within target. Patient highly compliant. Continue current plan.

historyTimeline.title: Regular Follow-up
historyTimeline.summary: HbA1c 7.1%, improving trend. Diet modification showing positive results.

llmSummary: Patient Divya Menon, 40F, demonstrates excellent Type 2 DM management with HbA1c of 6.8%. Glycemic control is exemplary. No significant complications. Low-risk profile. Continue current management strategy.
